chemistry.reaction_name: Continuous Flow Photochemical Synthesis of 3-Methyl-4-arylmethylene Isoxazole-5(4H)-ones
chemistry.bond_formed: C-C bond (Knoevenagel condensation) and isoxazole ring formation
chemistry.scope_summary: Eight compounds obtained with yields ranging from 30-96%. Aldehydes with electron-donating groups (OMe, OH, methylenedioxy) showed good results. Aldehydes with electron-withdrawing groups (Cl, NO2) failed to react. Best results with 3,4-dimethoxybenzaldehyde (96% yield).

batch_baseline.vessel: sealed 10 mL borosilicate glass reaction vessel

optimization_table.key_findings: 150 W halogen lamp gave best yield (90%) in 10 min, but 6 W UV lamp gave 77% yield in 6 h

light_source.position_relative_to_reactor: outer diameter 1.6 mm, inner diameter 0.8 mm

figures.caption: Figure 2.  Larval mortality profile for the 3-methyl-4-arylmethylene isoxazol-5(4H)-one derivatives based on the WHO guidelines.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Requires heating to 120°C and longer reaction times
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Continuous processing, improved heat/mass transfer, back pressure regulation
translation_logic.what_changed: Process mode from batch to flow, residence time increased from 10 to 20 min, concentration decreased from 1.0 M to 0.5 M
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: 150 W halogen lamp light source, water solvent, photochemical mechanism
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Elimination of high temperature heating requirements

figure_descriptions: Biological activity figure present but no process diagrams identified

conflicts: Pass1 indicates reactor scheme present but Pass4 found no process diagrams - only biological activity figure
notes: Well-documented photochemical flow synthesis with clear quantitative data. Missing some process visualization figures but comprehensive optimization data available.
